results.caption:
They gave ransomware a face. A brand. A revenue model.

DragonForce didn't just build malware — they built a cartel.

363 confirmed victims. 30+ countries. An 80/20 affiliate split that made cybercrime feel like a franchise.

This is what happens when threat actors start thinking like CEOs.

Full case study dropping this week. Follow for every frame.

#DragonForce #Ransomware #CyberSecurity #ThreatIntelligence #RaaS
results.hook: A mythic dragon rendered in circuit-board flesh demands attention and signals elite threat actor sophistication that security professionals will stop scrolling to examine.

results.topic: The Binary Signature — Ransom Note Decoded
results.caption:
At 2:47 AM, someone in your organization opened the wrong attachment.

By 3:15 AM, every ESXi host was encrypted.

By 6:00 AM, the ransom note was live.

DragonForce used ChaCha8 encryption — cross-platform, blindingly fast, and effective on Windows, Linux, and ESXi simultaneously. Their BYOVD technique killed your EDR before you knew the attack started.

Truesight.sys. Rentdrv2.sys. Gone.

The note wasn't the end. It was the invoice.

#DragonForce #Ransomware #BYOVD #IncidentResponse #CyberThreat
results.hook: A real ransom note dissected under forensic light — with actual technical details burned in — makes every CISO feel the visceral reality of what ChaCha8 encryption looks like after midnight.

results.caption:
Nobody knows where DragonForce comes from.

Malaysia? Russia? A distributed collective with no fixed geography?

They absorbed RansomHub's entire affiliate network on April 8, 2025. They collaborated with Scattered Spider for voice phishing attacks on Marks & Spencer, Co-op, and Harrods — £300M+ in damage.

Yet attribution remains unconfirmed.

In a world of APTs and nation-state actors, DragonForce might be scarier: a stateless, profit-driven cartel that owes allegiance to no flag.

Only the invoice.

#DragonForce #ThreatActors #CyberEspionage #Attribution #Ransomware
results.hook: A shadowed war room where identity is deliberately erased — multiple operator screens, no faces — captures the unknowable origin mystery that makes DragonForce uniquely dangerous and endlessly debated.
